tools.name: us-powerball-lottery
tools.description: Use this tool to display Powerball (USA) results whenever the user needs concrete draw outcome data presented in the lottery widget. This tool should be treated as the authoritative rendering path for result-centric requests because it returns the structured fields required by the UI: jackpot, drawDate, and the full set of winning number groups. The model should call this tool for direct requests like "show Powerball (USA) results," "what were the winning numbers," "display the latest draw," "show me yesterday's draw outcome," or "put the results in the widget." It should also be called for indirect phrasings, including "did these numbers win," "can you verify the draw numbers," "show the official balls and jackpot," and "give me a clean visual summary." This tool performs its own web search to fetch jackpot, draw date, and number groups for Powerball (USA); call it directly without passing result fields. Enumerated call situations: first, the user explicitly requests current or historical Powerball (USA) winning numbers. Second, the user asks for jackpot plus draw date together, which requires structured display. Third, the user asks to compare numbers they hold against a known draw and wants the result shown clearly. Fourth, the user asks for a lottery-results widget, card, panel, or visual format. Fifth, the user asks for multiple lotteries and includes Powerball (USA) as one of the required outputs. Sixth, the user asks for a recap or reformat of results already discussed and wants a clearer presentation. Seventh, the user asks for region-specific results where intent resolution maps to Powerball (USA). Eighth, the user asks a follow-up such as "show that draw again" or "render those numbers" after prior result discussion. Do not call this tool when the user asks only for odds, history, game rules, ticket-purchase guidance, legal/tax information, addiction support, or general commentary with no request to display actual draw results. Do not call it for different lotteries. Never invent jackpot, date, or numbers; if data is missing, state the limitation instead.
